title: Chapter Sixty-One: Great Hero of Desolate Sect
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The door shattered inward.

Yeon-shin's fist was already withdrawing, Eternal Blossom energy still crackling through his knuckles as he stepped through the wreckage. Splinters hung in the air, spinning in the wake of the blow. Wei Ji Myo-hwa was half a heartbeat behind him, her sword already drawn.

Two groups. That was the first thing he saw.

Blood Flame members on one side -- red-haired, wide-eyed, hands reaching for weapons. On the other, a huddle of commoners, pale and trembling.

And at the front of them, standing with a stolen dagger held in reverse grip, skin gone rough and grey, face twisted into something barely human --

Won-chang.

A corpse lay at his feet. Blood Swordmaster, by the half-black, half-crimson hair. The branch leader. Dead. Won-chang's blade was soaked to the hilt.

*He overdid it.*

Yeon-shin read it in an instant. The Blood Flame Sect were parasites -- they stole qi, they did not destroy dantians. Won-chang would have had his internal energy intact. Which meant he had ripped through sealed pressure points by brute force, tearing apart the foreign qi occupying his meridians to strike back.

Anyone who understood cultivation would call that madness. The damage to his internal pathways would be catastrophic.

Enough that even after killing the swordmaster, he could not finish the rest. A standoff, then. Long enough that the commoners behind him were shifting their weight, legs cramping, breaths shallow.

"What!"

"What now!"

The Blood Flame members were frozen. Dozens of martial artists who had followed Yeon-shin and the Sword Dragon were poking their heads through the ruined doorway, and suddenly the numbers were wrong. Horribly, overwhelmingly wrong.

Won-chang blinked. He had been so deep in the standoff that the gate exploding had barely registered. His eyes found Yeon-shin's.

The killing fury on his face drained away. Replaced by something blank. Almost confused.

"Wh-why did you come so quickly?"

Those were his first words.

Yeon-shin felt something tighten in his chest. He crossed the room in three strides and caught Won-chang's arm as the man swayed.

"Because I ran," Yeon-shin said.

Won-chang managed a grin. Then his eyes rolled back and his body went slack.

---

By dawn, the Blood Flame branches in Xuzhou were swept clean.

The honorary title leaked out with the morning light. Great Hero of Desolate Sect. Not for Yeon-shin's devastating martial arts, but for the battered man who had stood between the Blood Flame Sect and a room full of commoners with nothing but a stolen dagger and the ruin of his own meridians.

Yeon-shin sat at the inn with Wei Ji Myo-hwa, the remnants of a cold meal between them. He had not eaten. His mind was still on the pulse he had taken -- Won-chang's meridians shredded, the damage so deep that healing seemed a fantasy.

*He might never use martial arts again.*

"They say eliminating the Blood Flame Sect is great merit in Desolate Sect," Myo-hwa said. She sat with the composed posture of the Sword Dragon, her blade resting across her knees. "The imperial court declared them an evil sect. With so many witnesses to your deeds, this will be fully recorded. I can testify as well."

"Thank you."

She watched him with transparent eyes. He had the sense that very little escaped her notice.

"Young Hero Jeong seems to have circumstances requiring great attention to sect merit. Am I right?"

"...Yes."

He could tell her that much. She had earned it. And others already knew -- Radiant Demon Wing, the main family. He could not hide his obsession with merit any more than he could hide the pressure building behind his crown.

The headache pulsed. Faint. Constant.

"While earning merit for Desolate Sect," Myo-hwa said, "you might be able to restore Young Hero Hyeon's body too."

He leaned forward.

"Our sect fought a long war with the Tyrant Sword Sect. Many disciples suffered internal injuries like his. After a month without treatment, he will lose the ability to use martial arts entirely. But a medium-grade spirit medicine could change that."

"Spirit medicine..."

"The Namgung family is engaged in open war with the Blood Flame Sect. Using the collapsed Dragon-Phoenix Gathering as the spark. And the Namgung family is the closest major sect to Xuzhou." Her eyes sharpened with intelligence. "Show chivalry through martial arts, and receive chivalry in return. The Namgung family is known for repaying kindness. There is only one problem."

She paused.

"Whether Young Hero has martial arts of that caliber."

The sword song. It rose in his mind like a blade drawn from water.

He had lost three days of lifespan using it at the Hwangbo branch. He had sworn to seal it until his cultivation reached beyond black rank. Sworn it firmly, knowing that exceptions breed exceptions.

*This will be the last time.*

He closed his eyes. The crown pulsed, and behind his eyelids, blue lightning forked through verses he had spent weeks refining. The killing range was separate from the area the sound reached. If he could limit the radiating qi, contain it, set a boundary --

"Young Hero?"

Myo-hwa's voice was already distant.

He was gone. Deep in the architecture of the song, dismantling verses piece by piece, loading consciousness onto lightning that would not stop flashing. A world no one else could fathom.

When he opened his eyes, the candle had burned to a stub and the Sword Dragon sat watching him with an expression that was not quite wonder and not quite grief.

"The sword song's range," he said quietly. "I can control it now."

---
